name: Santuario del Tallat and Colina Grande de Vallbona — 100 summits of Catalonia
name_original: Santuario del Tallat y Colina Grande de Vallbona — 100 cimas de Cataluña
region_intro: Hiking around Vallbona De Les Monges offers diverse landscapes in the Urgell region of Lleida, situated at the foot of the Serra del Tallat. The area features a varied topography including hills like Tossal del Xifré and Tossal Gros, the highest point of the Serra del Tallat. Hikers can traverse pine forests, garrigues, and cultivated fields within the Maldanell valley, a tributary of the Corb river. This blend of natural and agricultural scenery provides a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Vallbona De Les Monges The most popular hiking route is Santuario del Tallat and Colina Grande de Vallbona — 100 summits of Catalonia, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route leads to the Santuari del Tallat, offering views from its 788-meter-high position. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Vallbona de les Monges, an easy 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This loop provides an accessible way to explore the immediate surroundings of Vallbona de les Monges, passing through varied local terrain. Local hikers also love the Santuario del Tallat – Montblanquet loop from Montblanquet, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through the Serra del Tallat and past Montblanquet, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Vallbona De Les Monges is defined by the Serra del Tallat mountain range, the fertile Maldanell valley, and a mix of pine forests and cultivated fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate ascents. The routes in Vallbona De Les Monges are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Vallbona De Les Monges's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Vallbona de les Monges?
A: Hiking in Vallbona de les Monges offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll traverse the varied topography of the Serra del Tallat, including hills like Tossal del Xifré and Tossal Gros. The routes often pass through a mosaic of pine forests and garrigues (shrublands), interspersed with cultivated fields of vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and cereals in the valleys, particularly along the Maldanell valley.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Vallbona de les Monges offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vallbona de les Monges&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.5 km path that provides an accessible way to explore the immediate surroundings and varied local terrain.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in history. A central attraction is the 12th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Vallbona&lt;/a&gt;, a Cistercian abbey that is also part of the Cistercian Route (GR-175). You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Tallat&lt;/a&gt;, a Gothic structure from the 13th century, perched atop a hill. For those interested in military history, the Ruta de la Línia de Defensa Republicana L2 explores Spanish Civil War constructions.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vallbona de les Monges&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14891716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Tallat – Montblanquet loop from Montblanquet&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Serra del Tallat.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Vallbona de les Monges?
A: The routes in Vallbona de les Monges are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the historical points of interest, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I find any scenic viewpoints or panoramic vistas on the hikes?
A: Absolutely. The Serra del Tallat is a prominent feature for scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930311478/santuario-del-tallat-and-colina-grande-de-vallbona-100-summits-of-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Tallat and Colina Grande de Vallbona&lt;/a&gt; route leads to the Santuari del Tallat, which offers spectacular panoramic views towards the Conca de Barberà from its 788-meter-high position.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are moderate, the region offers longer options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17511544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montblanquet – Santuario del Tallat loop from Rocallaura&lt;/a&gt; covers over 16 km and involves significant elevation gain, providing a more demanding experience for those seeking a longer trek.

Q: What is the Cistercian Route (GR-175) and how does it relate to Vallbona de les Monges?
A: The Cistercian Route (GR-175) is a significant 105 km circular path connecting the three most important Cistercian monasteries in Catalonia: Vallbona, Poblet, and Santes Creus. Vallbona de les Monges, with its historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Vallbona&lt;/a&gt;, serves as a key starting or ending point for sections of this historically rich long-distance trail.

Q: Are there any castles or ancient structures to explore nearby?
A: Yes, the area features several historical fortifications. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Milmanda Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an artistic and historical fortification. The nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4696023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guimerà&lt;/a&gt; also boasts a castle and a charming medieval atmosphere worth exploring.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vallbona de les Monges?
A: Vallbona de les Monges is suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the diverse landscapes of pine forests, garrigues, and cultivated fields. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter hiking is possible, though conditions may vary, especially at higher elevations in the Serra del Tallat.

Q: Are there any other notable landmarks besides the monastery and sanctuary?
A: Beyond the prominent monastery and sanctuary, you can also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3523125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Baixa&lt;/a&gt;, a monument with a history dating back to 1151, which adds another layer of historical interest to the region.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vallbona de les Monges?
A: There are over a dozen hiking trails available in Vallbona de les Monges, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. This includes easy loops around the town and more moderate ascents into the Serra del Tallat.
tips: The Tallat sanctuary is located on top of a 788-meter-high hill in the homonymous mountain range. It is Gothic in style and dates from the 13th century. History of the Civil War.
The tozal Gros de Vallbona is located at 804 meters above sea level. It is the highest summit of the Urgell and offers magnificent views of the Conca de Barberà.
